mission.title: Install and Configure Armada on Kubernetes
mission.description: Armada is a multi-cluster batch queuing system designed for high-throughput workloads on Kubernetes. Installing Armada allows you to efficiently manage and schedule jobs across multiple Kubernetes clusters, making it ideal for large-scale batch processing needs.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Armada Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add armada https://charts.armadaproject.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Armada using Helm
steps.description:
Install Armada in a new namespace called 'armada' with the specified version:
```bash
helm install armada armada/armada --namespace armada --create-namespace --version v0.20.34
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all pods are running in the 'armada' namespace:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure optimal resource usage, edit the Armada deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to edit the deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment armada --namespace armada
``` 
In the editor, add the following under the container spec:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "1000m"
    memory: "512Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up Prometheus monitoring
steps.description:
To enable monitoring with Prometheus, you can install the Prometheus operator and configure it to scrape metrics from Armada. First, install the Prometheus operator:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/main/bundle.yaml
```
Then, create a ServiceMonitor for Armada:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
  name: armada-monitor
  namespace: armada
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: armada
  endpoints:
  - port: metrics
    interval: 30s
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Armada will show all pods running in the 'armada' namespace when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace armada`. Additionally, you will have configured resource limits and set up Prometheus monitoring for your Armada instance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add armada https://charts.armadaproject.io
helm repo update
helm install armada armada/armada --namespace armada --create-namespace --version v0.20.34

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
